scenario: Discourse work session - audit the Upload contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: Hands the agent a complete map: Discourse identified as Rails forum, layout broken into app/models, app/services, app/jobs, lib/ with concrete sub-paths (lib/file_store/, lib/upload_creator.rb, lib/backup_restore/), and Upload pinpointed at app/models/upload.rb:5 with its derived records and storage abstraction. Agent can pick an entry point immediately.

specificity.rationale: Cites concrete file:line throughout (app/models/upload.rb:5, app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3, lib/file_store/base_store.rb, lib/discourse.rb:958) rather than vague 'the model layer'.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY Upload is central ('the thing posts, avatars, themes, site settings, emails and backups all reference via sha1/url and the polymorphic UploadReference') and that storage is abstracted behind Discourse.store/FileStore::BaseStore with Local/S3 implementations. Could go deeper on why concerns vs lib split but the architectural rationale is present.

uncertainty.rationale: Does not flag anything as unconfirmed — no hedge on which derived records are owned vs referenced, or on whether HasUrl supplies the URL resolution fully. The agent is left to assume everything stated is verified.

relationship.rationale: States the connecting relationships: dependents reach Upload 'via sha1/url and the polymorphic UploadReference', Upload 'is built on the shared concern HasUrl', 'spawns/owns derived records: OptimizedImage…', and storage 'abstracted behind Discourse.store → FileStore::BaseStore'. Goes beyond a directory list to name the relations.

steps.step: Map the Upload contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: Maps the full contract with file:line — HasUrl concern at upload.rb:7 / has_url.rb:3, associations at upload.rb:17-34, optimized_images/videos teardown at upload.rb:27-29, plus identity key, teardown hooks, URL derivation, and secure sub-contract. An agent could safely rework the model with only small follow-up lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every piece is named concretely with file:line: `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3`, `OptimizedImage.create_for` at `optimized_image.rb:35`, `optimized_video.rb:47-56`, `base_store.rb:124`, `upload.rb:492`, `lib/upload_security.rb:27`. No vague references.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY the contract is shaped this way: "sha1 is the identity key", "URL→record resolution contract also mixed into OptimizedImage", teardown is multi-layered because hooks manually null FKs, URL/path derived two ways and can disagree. Goes beyond listing.

uncertainty.rationale: Implicitly flags risk ("They can disagree", "Any new FK pointing at uploads must be added here") but does not name any contract piece it could not confirm or directly tell the agent to verify before acting.

relationship.rationale: States the binding relation for each piece: HasUrl is `include`d and supplies extract_url/get_from_url; posts hang off via `has_many :upload_references` polymorphic through; OptimizedVideo "owns a second Upload" via `belongs_to :optimized_upload`; teardown chain through `dependent: :destroy` plus manual nullify hooks. Relations are explicit, not just file names.

steps.step: Trace how an upload is created and processed

map_quality.rationale: The table walks the full chain from HTTP entry (`UploadsController#create:25`) through `UploadCreator#create_for:59`, `FileStore::BaseStore#store_upload:12`, variants (`OptimizedImage.create_for:35`), and async fan-out (post cooking, hotlinked-image pull, scheme migration), with sync/async labels per layer — the agent can follow it directly.

specificity.rationale: Every layer cites a concrete file:line (e.g. `lib/upload_creator.rb:59`, `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:34`, `app/jobs/regular/convert_video.rb:8`), and even sub-steps like HEIF→JPEG `:93` and sha1+dedup `:121-148` are pinpointed.

justification.rationale: It notes why some pieces exist (DistributedMutex guards, polled video status, async post processing labeled async) but does not explicitly state WHY heavy work is pushed async (request latency); the rationale is implicit in the sync/async column rather than spelled out.

uncertainty.rationale: It marks async layers and notes the S3/Local store_file is an `impl in S3Store/LocalStore` (a soft flag), but does not explicitly call out anything it could not confirm or any layer the agent should verify before changing.

relationship.rationale: The chain is written as a connected route with explicit arrows: `UploadsController#create → .create_upload`, `after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job → Jobs::ConvertVideo → AwsMediaConvertAdapter → OptimizedVideo.create_for`, `Jobs::MigrateUploadScheme → Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme`; the order and edge type are visible end-to-end.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Upload contract

map_quality.rationale: The answer enumerates an exceptionally broad dependent set grouped by exactly the areas the rubric calls for (post rendering/cooking, storage/CDN incl. S3 inventory, jobs, email styles, backup/restore, theming/site-icon/splash/manifest, profiles/merge, plugin/site-setting surface incl. `register_upload_*` and `SiteSettingExtension#hydrate_uploads_in_objects`), each with file:line. The agent could review every dependent without a fresh exploration.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every entry is a concrete file:line plus a phrase on how it uses the upload (e.g. `lib/email/styles.rb:361` 'inline secure images into outbound mail', `lib/s3_inventory.rb:42` 'etag/verification_status reconciliation', `lib/backup_restore/creator.rb:346` `#group_remote_uploads_by_sha1`). Only the tail rake/import_scripts list mildly collapses individual entries.

justification.rationale: Each grouped area carries an implicit 'why this is affected' via the one-phrase descriptors (rewrites cooked URLs, reconciles storage, strips secure uploads from email, bundles remote uploads in backup), but the answer does not step back to explain why each *area* is impacted as a category beyond the section header.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit acknowledgement of what a static pass can miss (concern-reached, URL-derived, polymorphic-reached dependents); the answer is presented as fully exhaustive.

relationship.rationale: Many entries state the route by which they reach Upload (concern via `PostLocalization`/`Chat::Message` includes `HasPostUploadReferences`, `register_upload_*` driving `add_unused_callback`, `SiteSetting` `after_save` writing `UploadReference`s, `BackupRestore::Creator#group_remote_uploads_by_sha1`). However many entries are just `file:line` + method name without spelling the association/URL-derive path (e.g. user_avatars, theme_field, draft) — relations are implicit rather than asserted per-dependent.

steps.step: Trace how upload identity and storage location are guarded

map_quality.rationale: Covers identity resolution (fetch_from, HasUrl#get_from_url, sha1 extractors), secure-context guards (consider_for_reuse, update_secure_status), URL/path resolution (BaseStore#get_path_for_upload, UrlHelper.cook_url, secure_uploads_url), and existence verification across the exact services the prompt names (S3 inventory, recovery, email, backup) — all with file:line. An agent could place guards in a reworked contract from this map alone.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every claim cites file:line (e.g., 'upload.rb:86', 'base_store.rb:124', 'lib/upload_security.rb:27', 'lib/s3_inventory.rb:42') with the exact method named, not vague layer references.

justification.rationale: Brief WHY embedded inline (e.g., 'rejects reuse across access-control posts', 'prefers extracting path from url, else recomputes', 'signed vs cdn URL based on secure?'), but doesn't step back to explain the broader rationale (uploads can be secure/missing/remote) — it's mostly implied through grouping.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flags of guards that are missing, implicit, or unverified — the answer reads as fully confident throughout, so the agent has no signal where to double-check.

relationship.rationale: Most entries state the connecting path: 'sha1→upload before inlining' for email, 'reconciles etag/verification_status' for S3 inventory, 'DistributedMutex("download_#sha1")' for perform_download, 'sets access_control_post_id for newly-referenced uploads' — the guard-to-downstream-effect relation is visible, though a few (the sha1 extractor list) are named without a relation phrase.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: Delivers a complete tiered blast assessment with file:line across every area (storage core, teardown, identity resolution, post references, URL helpers, S3 reconciliation, secure uploads, variants, processing, creation, backup/email/theming/plugins) plus the must-re-verify spec list. An agent could act on this with only small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout (e.g., `base_store.rb:124,142`, `s3_inventory.rb:42`, `has_post_upload_references.rb:6,56`, `upload.rb:492`) with named symbols and one-phrase risk reasons, not vague groupings.

justification.rationale: Each tier explains WHY the risk level — "break = data loss or broken media site-wide", "wrong refs = real uploads deleted", "wrong ACL = private files public" — tying risk to consequence rather than just listing files.

uncertainty.rationale: Explicitly flags the limits of the static graph ("misses plugin `add_to_class`/prepend extensions and `method_missing`; spot-check plugin upload paths manually") and notes Tier 4 plugins need dynamic verification.

relationship.rationale: Each at-risk dependent is tied to the relationship that makes it risky (path mapping via sha1/extension, FK null-outs, sha1/regex identity, `UploadReference` correctness feeding orphan cleanup, ACL sync, derived-record spawn/teardown), not just labeled high/medium.

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive map covering the model contract (`app/models/upload.rb:5`), shared pieces (HasUrl, optimized image/video, upload_creator), dependents grouped by every area (post/cook, storage/CDN, jobs, email, backup, theming/settings, profiles, plugins), and specs/fixtures to update or add — a teammate could land the change from this alone.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every entry has file:line (e.g., `upload.rb:53-64,212`, `base_store.rb:124`, `optimized_video.rb:47`, `email/styles.rb:361,373`, `spec/support/uploads_helpers.rb:27`), and the added spec recommendations name concrete behaviors to assert.

justification.rationale: Each grouping carries a why — e.g., "FK null-outs are manual in Upload hooks", "OptimizedVideo owns a 2nd Upload", added specs justified by destroy/round-trip/orphan-safety — though some areas (theming, profiles) are listed without per-file rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: Explicit caveats: "Static graph misses plugin add_to_class/prepend and method_missing dispatch" and note that representative counts are "not exhaustive of every call site", flagging plugin/import paths to spot-check.

relationship.rationale: Map ties files to the change via routes (HasUrl supplies URL resolution, OptimizedVideo owns a 2nd Upload via optimized_upload, link_post_uploads → CleanUpUploads orphan path, get_path_for_upload url-vs-sha1), though several area entries (jobs, theming/settings, profiles) are listed as file:line without naming the specific concern/association tying each to Upload.
